entries.description: Beacon privacy scandal (2007): Facebook launched Beacon, which automatically shared users' purchases and activities from partner sites without clear consent, leading to widespread backlash over unauthorized data sharing and privacy violations.
entries.causation_evidence: NBC timeline of Zuckerberg apologies; court and regulatory records

entries.description: 2011 FTC settlement: Facebook settled with the FTC for deceiving users by making private information public without notice, sharing data with advertisers contrary to promises, and failing to honor privacy settings. This led to ongoing consent decree and oversight.

Cambridge Analytica scandal (2014–2018): Data from up to 87 million Facebook users was improperly harvested via a personality quiz app and used for political profiling and targeted ads (including 2016 US election and Brexit). Facebook knew of issues since 2015 but took limited action. Led to major congressional hearings, Zuckerberg testimony, and record FTC fine.
entries.causation_evidence: NYT/Guardian reporting; FTC $5B settlement 2019; congressional records

entries.description: Instagram mental health harms and whistleblower revelations (2018–2021+): Internal research (including on teen girls) showed Instagram worsened body image issues, eating disorders, anxiety, and depression for a significant portion of young users. Meta buried or limited follow-up on findings showing causal links (e.g., deactivation studies reducing depression/anxiety). Frances Haugen whistleblower exposed algorithmic promotion of divisive content.
entries.causation_evidence: Wall Street Journal Files; Frances Haugen testimony; 2025–2026 court filings on Project Mercury

entries.description: Multistate lawsuits over harms to children (2023–2026): 40+ attorneys general sued Meta for designing addictive features on Facebook/Instagram that harm youth mental health, expose minors to sexual predators, and lower self-esteem. Internal documents showed knowledge of risks. Led to 2026 landmark trials in California and New Mexico where juries found Meta liable for addiction and harm; Zuckerberg testified.
entries.causation_evidence: Multistate AG lawsuits; 2026 jury verdicts (CA/NM); Reuters court filings

entries.description: Role in election interference and misinformation (2016 onward): Platforms amplified fake news, Russian disinformation, and divisive content during US elections and globally. Later decisions on content moderation (including reduced fact-checking in 2025 and past suppression debates) contributed to ongoing polarization. Zuckerberg faced multiple apologies and congressional scrutiny.
entries.causation_evidence: Intelligence reports on 2016 interference; internal documents; congressional hearings

entries.description: Ongoing content moderation shifts and accountability gaps (2024–2026): Meta reduced third-party fact-checking, shifted to community notes model, and faced criticism for allowing more toxic material while earlier burying harm research. Multiple trials and filings alleged deliberate design choices prioritizing engagement over safety.

Chan Zuckerberg Initiative pledge and philanthropy (2015–2026): Zuckerberg and Priscilla Chan pledged 99% of their Facebook/Meta shares (initially ~$45B, growing significantly) to the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative focused on advancing human potential, equality, medical research (e.g., $3B+ commitments), education, and global health initiatives. Includes Biohub, disease eradication efforts, and other grants.
entries.causation_evidence: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative announcements and reports; public letters

entries.description: Creation and global connectivity via Facebook/Meta platforms (2004–2026): Built tools connecting billions of people worldwide for communication, business, and information sharing, enabling economic opportunities, social movements, and access in developing regions despite controversies.
entries.causation_evidence: Meta annual reports; user growth statistics
